name: Metro Línea 1 — Estadio Norte
description: Direct line to Centro Histórico and main rail station. Station is north of the stadium.
surgeNote: Extremely busy for ~45 minutes after the final whistle. If your seat is on the west side, Línea 3 from Gate D is usually faster.

name: Metro Línea 3 — Avenida Poniente
description: Connects to the west of the city with interchange to Línea 1 downtown.
surgeNote: The quieter post-match option — queues are typically half those at Estadio Norte.

name: Bus 45 — Airport Express
description: Limited-stop service to Guadalajara International Airport (~40 min).
surgeNote: Extra services run for 90 minutes after full-time. Allow 3+ hours before any flight.

name: Bus 12 — City Loop
description: Circular route serving hotels district and Chapultepec nightlife zone.
surgeNote: Busy but moves quickly; stands are 5 minutes south of Gate C.

name: Bus 88 — Park & Ride
description: Shuttle to the remote Park & Ride lots (free with match ticket).
surgeNote: Runs continuously after the match until lots are clear.

name: Parking Lot P1 (East)
description: Pre-booked match-day parking, 900 spaces. Entry from Avenida Oriente.
surgeNote: Exit queues peak 20–50 minutes after full-time; waiting 30 minutes roughly halves your queue time.

name: Parking Lot P2 (South)
description: Pre-booked parking with accessible bays nearest Gate C.
surgeNote: Accessible-bay holders may use the priority exit lane southbound.
